mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms sustained economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315.0, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 315.0, or inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy after 2023.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the joint signal shows negative alpha during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply chain contraction and reduced factory utilization, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle observations, or the signal loses predictive power when inventory levels are high.
